intro: Migrating from TypeScript to C# translates frontend web skills into Microsoft's enterprise-ready, component-based backend language. Developers frequently transition to C# to build secure enterprise cloud services, game backends with Unity, or large-scale web services within the .NET ecosystem. The shift moves developers from structural type matching rules into a rigid, fully integrated nominal runtime framework.

diffs.from: Compile-time structural mechanics that erase interface footprints during execution.
diffs.to: Strict nominal static typing verified at compile time and preserved at runtime via Reflection and Generics metadata.

diffs.from: C-style presentation allowing loose structural models, implicit arrow constructs, and module configurations.
diffs.to: Formal object-oriented layout utilizing explicit type definitions, properties, and strict namespace grouping frameworks.

diffs.from: V8 engine automatic reference handling and generation scanning scripts.
diffs.to: Common Language Runtime (CLR) multi-generational garbage collector optimized for enterprise server configurations.

diffs.from: Single-thread event loop processing micro-tasks, Promise blocks, and async patterns.
diffs.to: Task-based Asynchronous Pattern (TAP) using thread-pool schedulers alongside native async/await keywords.

diffs.from: Basic operations primitives; depends on npm additions for file system structures or web utilities.
diffs.to: Massive, high-performance base class framework (.NET BCL) covering data access, cryptography, and network sockets.

examples.title: Strong Properties and Model Configurations
examples.description: Creating read-only property bindings on models.

examples.title: Asynchronous Task Implementations
examples.description: Fetching remote data streams using non-blocking asynchronous calls.

tips.title: Map LINQ over Array Iteration Paths
tips.body: Replace continuous TypeScript array methods like `.filter()` or `.map()` with C#'s Language Integrated Query (LINQ) extensions, using `.Where()` and `.Select()` inside the `System.Linq` namespace.

tips.title: Enforce Value Type vs Reference Type Rules
tips.body: Understand that C# makes distinct structural distinctions between value models (`struct`) and standard heap references (`class`). Structs are copied completely during assignment actions, whereas classes pass reference handles.

tips.title: Utilize Tasks for Parallel Computations
tips.body: C# async execution tasks run on top of background thread-pool infrastructure. Unlike single-threaded TypeScript async execution loops, concurrent tasks in C# can execute simultaneously across multiple hardware processors.

tips.title: Leverage Capitalization and Formatting Rules
tips.body: Adhere to Microsoft naming standards. Method declarations, property references, and class definitions use PascalCase (e.g., `CalculateTotal`), whereas internal variable pointers use standard camelCase format bindings.
